title: Living in Boulder vlog 8 -  17 -  2017: 19.5mi 1:40:25 Time 2,464ft. Eusebius.

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs after a bike ride in Boulder on August 19, 2017, covering 19.5 miles with 2,464ft elevation. He recounts a ride up Flagstaff where his bike computer went into low-battery sleep mode, then discusses working on notes about ancient books (Eusebius), and reflects on the steep mountain terrain and his plans for tomorrow's ride.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: glad he did it despite the battery issue; found the steep 14% grade impressive
working through Eusebius as a productive afternoon activity he enjoys
fascinated by the terrain after having only looked at it on Google for a long time

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder V-Log. It's 2.11 a.m. on August 19th. So, this is a V-Log for the 18th.
So, today I went on a ride in the afternoon and Flagstaff. I was going to go up Flagstaff and then down the other side to the end of the road and then come back.
Um, but when I got to the top of the hill, um, it said that my battery was low and it wasn't giving me any data and I thought maybe it wouldn't, um, record my ride.
So, I just went right home. But, um, but it did record the whole ride. So, I just went into sleep mode and managed to be able to transfer the information.
So, that's why I didn't, and then I could have written more. I was like four when I came back, but I wanted to do, uh, some work on trying to finish these ancient books.
There's just so many writers. Eusebius is prolific. That's what I'm working on now.
And then I went on a ride with my mom and then came back and was working on him until early, um, on one of his books.
And so, it was time consuming. And so, now I'm trying to finish it up. I think it's just little ones.
Yeah, and then I'll zoom through and we'll see if we get to somebody else. It's super, you know, complicated.
You know, lots of chapters and books. Um, but, yeah, I haven't been listening to anything really. Um, I'm, uh, I'm too high.
Coming up on the end of the smoke in the weed while I ride the bike. Um, it's really fun to ride the bike stone, though.
Um, fascinating to travel around the mountains after looking at him on Google Earth for so long.
Um, so, uh, yeah.
It's, uh, Flagstaff is super, super, uh, steep.
It was 14%. That's the steepest road that I've been on on my ride on my bike.
It's more than any part of, uh, Sunshine or Old Stage.
Or, uh, yeah. Um, but, uh, yeah, I think I'll do that tomorrow.
I might start by doing, uh, In-Car first, though.
I could just do as many hills as I can.
And I consider In-Car the first. That's a little, a hill.
So, I would have today, but I forgot.
I've been forgetting to put on my, uh, Heartbeat Monitor, too.
Hopefully I'll remember that tomorrow.
Um, okay. So, that's enough talking.
